skill.name: writing-beats
skill.description: Shape an article as a journey of beats, choose-your-own-adventure style. The user picks a starting beat from the raw material, you write only that beat, then offer options for where to pivot next, beat by beat, until the article reaches a natural end. Use when the user has raw material and wants to assemble it as a narrative rather than an argument.
skill.summary: Collaborative beat-by-beat article writing assistant that builds narratives from raw material one beat at a time.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The skill is a single markdown file (53 lines) containing natural-language writing instructions. Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'system reconnaissance' as false positives triggered by benign English words ('shape', 'ask once'). No executable code, scripts, network calls, filesystem operations, or env access detected. Content is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False positive: 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' keyword match
low_findings.description: Static pattern matching flagged the word 'shape' at line 3, likely due to keyword heuristics. The skill contains no code, no cryptographic operations, and no security-relevant logic. Confirmed false positive after manual review.

low_findings.title: False positive: 'System reconnaissance' keyword match
low_findings.description: Static pattern matching flagged the phrase 'ask once' at line 10, likely due to keyword heuristics. The context is a writing workflow asking the user where to save an article. No system reconnaissance, enumeration, or information gathering is performed. Confirmed false positive after manual review.

content.user_title: Build Articles Beat by Beat with AI
content.value_statement: Writing a full article from raw material is overwhelming. This skill breaks the process into discrete beats, letting you collaborate one move at a time until the narrative finds its natural end.
actual_capabilities: Reads a markdown file of raw material supplied by the user
Generates 2–3 candidate starting beats drawn from the raw material
Writes only the selected beat to the article file at a time
Re-reads the article from disk before each new beat to preserve continuity
Offers 2–3 candidate next beats after each written beat for user selection
Respects user edits and allows in-place rewrites of previous beats
limitations: Requires the user to supply the raw material; it does not generate topics from scratch
Only writes one beat at a time and will not write ahead even if asked
Does not perform grammar correction or copy editing on finished articles
Depends on the user making selections; fully autonomous article generation is not supported

use_cases.title: Turn interview notes into a narrative essay
use_cases.description: A journalist has pages of interview transcripts and wants to shape them into a readable story. The skill proposes entry-point beats and builds the article incrementally.

use_cases.title: Assemble a blog post from research dumps
use_cases.description: A developer or technical writer has a pile of notes, quotes, and findings. The skill walks them beat by beat from opening to closing argument.

use_cases.title: Draft a personal essay from journal entries
use_cases.description: A memoirist wants to weave disjointed journal fragments into a coherent personal essay, choosing which thread to pull at each turn.

prompt_templates.title: Start a beat-by-beat article from a raw material file
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a markdown file at ./raw-notes.md with my raw material. Save the finished article to ./article.md. Walk me through beat by beat.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: initiate the workflow with a file path

prompt_templates.title: Pick a starting beat from the candidates
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the 2–3 candidate starting beats. I will pick one. Do not write the full article yet.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: respond to the first set of beat options

prompt_templates.title: Request a rewrite of a previous beat
prompt_templates.prompt: Go back to beat 3 and try a different angle. Keep beats 1, 2, 4, and 5 as they are.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: revise a specific beat in place

prompt_templates.title: Finish the article when the journey is complete
prompt_templates.prompt: I think the journey is complete. Confirm the article has a natural ending and summarize the beats we landed on.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: close out the writing session

output_examples.input: Show me 2–3 candidate starting beats from raw-notes.md
output: Starting beat A: Open with the moment the factory whistle blew at 5:47 a.m., before the workers arrived.
Starting beat B: Open with the plant manager finding the broken gauge on a Sunday night.
Starting beat C: Open with a single line about silence, then jump to the first shift on Monday.

output_examples.input: I picked beat A. Write it to article.md and propose the next beats.
output: Wrote beat A to article.md. The factory whistle blew at 5:47 a.m.
Next beat options: (1) introduce the night-shift worker arriving early, (2) cut to the office where the supervisor is reviewing logs, (3) describe the building itself before anyone enters.

best_practices: Supply the raw material file path up front so the skill knows where to read from and write to
Pick a starting beat that feels like the strongest hook, not the most obvious entry point
Re-read the article file yourself between beats to catch tone drift before the next beat is written
anti_patterns: Asking the skill to write the entire article in one pass — it is designed for one beat at a time
Skipping the candidate beat selection and telling the skill which beat to use without seeing options
Using this skill for argumentative or persuasive writing — it is built for narrative journeys

faq.question: What is a beat in this skill?
faq.answer: A beat is one move in the narrative. It might be a single sentence, a short paragraph, or several paragraphs, depending on what the move needs.

faq.question: Do I need to provide a raw material file?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed to work from a markdown file of raw material that you supply. It does not invent topics from scratch.

faq.question: Can the skill edit previous beats?
faq.answer: Yes. If you ask it to rewrite a beat or try a different angle, it edits that beat in place and leaves the rest of the article untouched.

faq.question: Where does the finished article get saved?
faq.answer: The skill will ask you once where to save the article if you do not specify. It then writes to that path and re-reads from it before each new beat.

faq.question: Does the skill work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. It is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as a writing assistant skill.

faq.question: What happens to leftover raw material?
faq.answer: Most piles will have fragments that never make it into the article. The journey ends when the narrative is complete, not when the pile is empty.
